tier2_labels.1: sorghum panicle with no ergot, no honeydew, completely clean healthy panicle
tier2_labels.2: sorghum panicle with slight ergot infection, few small honeydew droplets on 1-10% of spikelets
tier2_labels.3: sorghum panicle with moderate ergot infection, honeydew droplets on 11-25% of spikelets
tier2_labels.4: sorghum panicle with heavy ergot infection, extensive honeydew on 26-50% of spikelets, visible mold
tier2_labels.5: sorghum panicle with severe ergot infection, entire panicle covered in honeydew, over 50% affected, sclerotia forming

tier2_labels.1: healthy sorghum leaf with no anthracnose lesions
tier2_labels.2: sorghum leaf with few small anthracnose lesions, 1-10% leaf area affected
tier2_labels.3: sorghum leaf with moderate anthracnose, lesions on 11-25% of leaf area
tier2_labels.4: sorghum leaf with severe anthracnose, 26-50% leaf area covered in lesions
tier2_labels.5: sorghum leaf heavily damaged by anthracnose, over 50% leaf area with coalescing lesions

tier2_labels.1: sorghum field plot with no visible panicles or all panicles still in boot stage, no flowering
tier2_labels.2: sorghum field plot with less than 25 percent of plants showing panicles with extruded yellow anthers, most plants not yet flowering
tier2_labels.3: sorghum field plot with approximately 50 percent of plants showing yellow anthers on panicles, half of the plot is flowering
tier2_labels.4: sorghum field plot with more than 75 percent of plants flowering, most panicles have visible anthers, some starting to dry
tier2_labels.5: sorghum field plot fully past flowering, all panicles done with anthesis, grain filling has begun, no fresh anthers visible

tier2_labels.1: very loose open sorghum panicle with widely spaced branches
tier2_labels.2: loose sorghum panicle with visible gaps between branches
tier2_labels.3: medium compact sorghum panicle with moderate branch density
tier2_labels.4: compact sorghum panicle with tightly packed branches
tier2_labels.5: very compact dense sorghum panicle, cylindrical shape, no gaps

tier2_labels.1: sorghum panicle with very poor seed set, less than 20% of florets filled, mostly empty glumes
tier2_labels.2: sorghum panicle with poor seed set, 20-40% of florets filled
tier2_labels.3: sorghum panicle with moderate seed set, 40-60% of florets filled
tier2_labels.4: sorghum panicle with good seed set, 60-80% of florets filled
tier2_labels.5: sorghum panicle with excellent seed set, over 80% of florets filled, plump grains throughout

tier2_labels.1: healthy sorghum plant with no disease symptoms, clean tissue
tier2_labels.2: sorghum plant with slight disease symptoms, 1-10% of tissue affected
tier2_labels.3: sorghum plant with moderate disease symptoms, 11-25% of tissue affected
tier2_labels.4: sorghum plant with heavy disease symptoms, 26-50% of tissue affected
tier2_labels.5: sorghum plant with severe disease, over 50% of tissue affected, extensive damage

tier2_labels.1: healthy sorghum leaf with no rust pustules, clean green leaf surface
tier2_labels.2: sorghum leaf with few small rust pustules, orange-brown spots on 1-10% of leaf area
tier2_labels.3: sorghum leaf with moderate rust infection, orange-brown pustules on 11-25% of leaf area
tier2_labels.4: sorghum leaf with heavy rust infection, dense orange-brown pustules covering 26-50% of leaf
tier2_labels.5: sorghum leaf severely damaged by rust, over 50% covered with coalescing orange-brown pustules, leaf tissue dying

tier2_labels.1: healthy sorghum panicle with clean grain, no visible mold or discoloration
tier2_labels.2: sorghum panicle with slight grain mold, small patches of fuzzy mold on 1-10% of grains
tier2_labels.3: sorghum panicle with moderate grain mold, visible mold growth on 11-25% of grains
tier2_labels.4: sorghum panicle with heavy grain mold, extensive fuzzy mold covering 26-50% of grains, discoloration
tier2_labels.5: sorghum panicle severely affected by grain mold, over 50% of grains covered, grain deteriorating

tier2_labels.1: healthy sorghum panicle with no smut, clean grain
tier2_labels.2: sorghum panicle with slight covered kernel smut, 1-10% of grains replaced by dark smut sori
tier2_labels.3: sorghum panicle with moderate covered smut, 11-25% of grains replaced by smut masses
tier2_labels.4: sorghum panicle with heavy covered smut, 26-50% of grains replaced by dark spore masses
tier2_labels.5: sorghum panicle severely affected by covered smut, over 50% of grains destroyed and replaced by black spore masses

tier2_labels.1: healthy sorghum panicle with normal grain development, no smut
tier2_labels.2: sorghum panicle with slight head smut, small dark spore mass visible on 1-10% of panicle
tier2_labels.3: sorghum panicle with moderate head smut, black spore masses replacing 11-25% of the panicle
tier2_labels.4: sorghum panicle with heavy head smut, large black spore masses replacing 26-50% of panicle structure
tier2_labels.5: sorghum panicle almost entirely replaced by black head smut spore mass, over 50% destroyed

tier2_labels.1: healthy sorghum panicle with no loose smut, normal grain development
tier2_labels.2: sorghum panicle with slight loose smut, small amount of dark powdery spores on 1-10% of panicle
tier2_labels.3: sorghum panicle with moderate loose smut, powdery black spores on 11-25% of panicle
tier2_labels.4: sorghum panicle with heavy loose smut, 26-50% of panicle covered in loose dark spore masses
tier2_labels.5: sorghum panicle severely affected by loose smut, over 50% of panicle is powdery black spore mass, most grain destroyed
